title: Calligraphy for left-handed writers
sources: Edward Johnston, Writing and Illuminating and Lettering (1906) — pen-hold and pen-angle; broadly documented in modern practice
notes: Reading article on adapting broad-edge calligraphy tools and posture for left-handed writers. Covers: underwriter vs overwriter hand position, the left-hand oblique nib holder, rotating the paper (the key adaptation for a natural stroke direction), nib angle compensations. Also notes that Italic with a pointed pen is more naturally accommodating for left-handers than Johnston Foundational.
